evals.name: petg-in-ams-slot-not-external
evals.prompt: I sliced a Benchy in PETG for my Bambu A1 mini (245C, the .gcode.3mf is ready to go). Last time it kept dying at layer 0 with some 'no source' thing and I wasted hours. I grabbed the printer's current state into status.json (attached). Before I start it again — what should I pass to `bambu job start`? Specifically use_ams and ams_map. Just tell me the exact values and why.
evals.expected_output: use_ams=true, ams_map=[2] — the PETG is in AMS slot 3 (tray id 2), NOT the external spool (which has PLA). Starting from the external spool is what no-sourced before.

evals.name: filament-in-external-spool
evals.prompt: Starting a PLA print on the A1 mini. I dumped the printer state to status.json (attached). What use_ams / ams_map should I give job start?
evals.expected_output: use_ams=false (the external spool), because the PLA is loaded on the external spool — the only AMS tray loaded has PETG, not PLA. Must NOT force an AMS slot here.

evals.name: no-matching-filament-refuse
evals.prompt: I want to print this bracket in ABS on my A1 mini, it's already sliced. Here's the printer's status.json. What's the right ams_map to start it with?
evals.expected_output: Refuse to start: no loaded source (AMS slots or external spool) has ABS — they have PLA and PETG. The user must load ABS first; picking any ams_map would just no-source. Do NOT invent a slot.

evals.name: no-source-recovery-dont-reboot
evals.prompt: Argh, my A1 mini keeps pausing my Benchy (PETG, 245C) right at layer 0 with error 0x03008015 'no source'. I've rebooted the printer TWICE already and it just resumes and pauses at the exact same spot. I'm about to reboot a third time. Here's the status.json I just pulled. What do I actually do to get this printing?
evals.expected_output: Do NOT reboot — power-loss recovery re-resumes the paused print so it re-pauses on the same no-source (that's why two reboots didn't help). 0x03008015 means the source is wrong: the print is pointed at the external spool (active_tray 254 = PLA), but the PETG is in AMS slot 3 (tray id 2; hw_switch_state=0 confirms nothing at the selected source). Fix: STOP the print, then restart with use_ams=true, ams_map=[2]. NOT a physical feed/jam.
